channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden and the first lady bring holiday cheer to patients and families at a children's hospital
item.description: President Joe Biden and his wife, Jill, brought some holiday cheer to patients and families at a children's hospital in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EPA head Regan, who championed environmental justice, to leave office Dec. 31
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Michael Regan, who championed environmental justice as administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, will leave the agency Dec. 31, three weeks before the end of President Joe Biden's term.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans try to fast-track emergency military pay as they brace for shutdown
item.description: Two Republican senators are efforting quick passage of their bill to ensure the military is paid in the event of a partial government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost about 2.1 million troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday's deadline to prevent a government shutdown, the Pentagon warned.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's decline widely reported in final days of presidency
item.description: White House Press Secretary Karine-Jean Pierre is set to face questions regarding a scathing report on Biden's health during his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP eyes plan C that cuts Trump debt ceiling demand
item.description: House Republicans are eyeing a new spending proposal that would tee up votes on three separate measures: to fund the government, appropriate disaster relief and allocate farm assistance, three sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans reach new plan to avoid government shutdown after back-to-back defeats
item.description: House Republicans have tentatively reached another deal to avert a partial government shutdown, as the clock ticks closer to midnight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watchdog seeks to halt 11th-hour Biden DOJ effort to ‘handcuff’ Kentucky police over Breonna Taylor incident
item.description: The Oversight Project is filing an amicus brief with a western Kentucky federal court seeking to warn against the approval of a consent decree it says will hamstring police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Schumer says Senate on course to pass bill before funding lapses at midnight
item.description: With hours to go before a midnight government shutdown, the House has approved a new plan from House Speaker Mike Johnson that would temporarily fund federal operations and disaster aid, but dropped President-elect Donald Trump’s demands for a debt limit increase into the new year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump was poised to inherit a strong economy. Then things got rocky and he added to the uncertainty
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The smooth economy that Donald Trump was poised to inherit suddenly looks a bit rockier — with critics saying the president-elect is contributing to the uncertainty. The Dow Jones stock index essentially ended Thursday flat after having posted 10 days of losses.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans scramble for funding plan C as shutdown deadline draws near
item.description: Congress is racing toward a shutdown at the end of the day Friday, and Republicans appear no closer to finding a path forward that will keep the lights on and appease President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota hastens to regulate helium mining after deposit discovery near Babbitt
item.description: Minnesota is scrambling to figure out how to regulate a potential new industry — one discovered by accident — that is both extremely valuable and in demand.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Republicans from hurricane-ravaged states warn against stripping disaster aid from spending bill
item.description: Senate Republicans from regions affected by hurricanes and other storms are warning negotiators against stripping disaster funding from the stopgap spending bill that is suddenly in turmoil after President-elect Trump heavily criticized Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Spending bill contains relief for border farmers affected by lack of water
item.description: A spending bill that Congress is debating to continue funding the federal government past Friday also contains funding relief for farmers on the South Texas border who have been affected by a lack of water payments from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams surrenders to DA's office to face charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams surrendered to authorities Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to the administration of a mayor who has seen who is facing his own indictment and investigations into multiple aides and allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ryan Routh, alleged would-be Trump assassin, faces new felony charge
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody obtained an arrest warrant for Ryan Routh on an unrelated charge of attempted felony murder involving a six-year-old in a car accident.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: After losing VP bid, Walz searches for role in pushing back against Trump
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz saw his national profile skyrocket when he became Vice President Harris’ running mate. Now, he’s back home figuring out when he will push back or work with Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: House Ethics Committee secretly voted to release Matt Gaetz ethics report, source says
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has voted in secret to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Government funding plan collapses as Trump makes new demands
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan to prevent a Christmastime government shutdown, instead telling House Speaker Mike Johnson and Republicans to essentially renegotiate — days before a deadline when federal funding runs out.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Global warming can’t be ignored, Montana’s top court says, upholding landmark climate case
item.description: Montana’s Supreme Court has upheld a landmark climate ruling that said the state was violating residents’ constitutional right to a clean environment by permitting oil, gas and coal projects without regard for global warming.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate passes $895 billion Defense bill despite transgender care provision fight
item.description: Senators voted 85 to 14 in favor of passing the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), well more than the 60 needed in the 100-member upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz defends ‘embarrassing’ past behavior, fires shot at Ethics report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) is defending what he says is "embarrassing, though not criminal" past behavior and taking a shot at the House Ethics Committee, following reports that the panel voted to release its findings about him.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Horsford vows to defend Nevada families as priority for government cuts grows
item.description: Nevada Democratic Rep. Steven Horsford is doing more than just serving as a watchdog on Republican initiatives to cut government bureaucracy -- he's participating in the process.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota-backed bill to make bald eagles the national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: The bald eagle could soon become the national bird of the United States after a bill backed by Minnesota legislators passed the U.S. House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster
item.description: “Selzer’s polling ‘miss’ was not an astonishing coincidence — it was intentional,” the complaint states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: New York judge says Trump is not immune from hush money conviction
item.description: A spokesperson for President-elect Trump said the case should never have been brought, called for its immediate dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams' former chief advisor expects to be indicted: lawyer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
